title: Introduction to Infectious Diseases
content:
Infectious diseases are illnesses caused by bacteria, viruses, fungi, or parasites.
These diseases can spread quickly within herds or flocks.
Early detection saves lives and improves productivity.
Media: 1-min animation: “What Are Infectious Diseases?”
sms_content: Infectious diseases are illnesses caused by bacteria, viruses, fungi, or parasites. These diseases can spread quickly within herds or flocks. Early detection saves lives and improves productivity. Media: 1-min animation: “What Are Infectious Diseases?”

title: How Infectious Diseases Spread
content:
Diseases spread through direct contact, dirty housing, contaminated food/water, insects, and tools.
Sick animals can infect healthy ones rapidly.
Infographic: Transmission routes (animal ↔ animal, feed, water, equipment).
sms_content: Diseases spread through direct contact, dirty housing, contaminated food/water, insects, and tools. Sick animals can infect healthy ones rapidly. Infographic: Transmission routes (animal ↔ animal, feed, water, equipment).

title: Common Signs & Symptoms
content:
Fever, loss of appetite, coughing, diarrhea, wounds, sudden death.
Isolation behavior is a warning sign.
Activity: “Spot the symptom” image identification.
sms_content: Fever, loss of appetite, coughing, diarrhea, wounds, sudden death. Isolation behavior is a warning sign. Activity: “Spot the symptom” image identification.

title: Major Infectious Diseases (Examples)
content:
Anthrax, foot-and-mouth disease, brucellosis, rabies, PPR, Newcastle disease.
Each disease has unique signs every farmer should learn.
Media: Short audio summaries per disease.
sms_content: Anthrax, foot-and-mouth disease, brucellosis, rabies, PPR, Newcastle disease. Each disease has unique signs every farmer should learn. Media: Short audio summaries per disease.

title: Preventing Disease Spread
content:
Clean and disinfect housing regularly.
Provide clean drinking water.
Avoid sharing tools between sick and healthy animals.
Infographic: Daily hygiene checklist.
sms_content: Clean and disinfect housing regularly. Provide clean drinking water. Avoid sharing tools between sick and healthy animals. Infographic: Daily hygiene checklist.

title: Vaccination & Deworming
content:
Vaccines prevent major infectious diseases.
Deworming reduces parasite load and reduces spread.
Follow a regular schedule with a veterinary professional.
Media: Vet officer video on vaccination routines.
sms_content: Vaccines prevent major infectious diseases. Deworming reduces parasite load and reduces spread. Follow a regular schedule with a veterinary professional. Media: Vet officer video on vaccination routines.

title: Quarantine & Isolation
content:
Isolate new animals for 10–14 days before mixing with the herd.
Keep sick animals separately until recovery.
Checklist: How to set up a simple quarantine pen.
sms_content: Isolate new animals for 10–14 days before mixing with the herd. Keep sick animals separately until recovery. Checklist: How to set up a simple quarantine pen.

title: Zoonotic Diseases (One Health)
content:
Some diseases (e.g., anthrax, brucellosis, rabies) pass from animals to humans.
Wear gloves, wash hands, cook meat well, boil milk.
Comic/story: “Why One Health matters.”
sms_content: Some diseases (e.g., anthrax, brucellosis, rabies) pass from animals to humans. Wear gloves, wash hands, cook meat well, boil milk. Comic/story: “Why One Health matters.”

title: Reporting & Seeking Help
content:
Report sudden deaths or strange symptoms immediately.
Early veterinary help reduces loss and saves herd health.
Activity: “When should I call a vet?” quiz.
sms_content: Report sudden deaths or strange symptoms immediately. Early veterinary help reduces loss and saves herd health. Activity: “When should I call a vet?” quiz.

title: Record Keeping for Disease Control
content:
Record symptoms, treatments, deaths, vaccinations, and feed changes.
Helps detect disease patterns early.
Template: Simple farm health logbook.
💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning)
Each SMS is ≤ 480 characters and accessible via Care4Animals SMS codes (e.g., DISEASE1–DISEASE10).
🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes
Languages: English, Luganda, Runyankole, Acholi, Kiswahili
Audience: Farmers, para-vets, village health teams, agriculture students
Channels:
Care4Animals mobile app (videos, quizzes, infographics)
SMS offline learning (short lessons)
Partners: Bugema University, district veterinary offices, community radio
Standards: WOAH (OIE) One Health, FAO animal health guidelines
Goal: Reduce livestock mortality and improve welfare through early detection and prevention of infectious diseases.
sms_content: Record symptoms, treatments, deaths, vaccinations, and feed changes. Helps detect disease patterns early. Template: Simple farm health logbook. 💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning) Each SMS is ≤ 480 characters and accessible via Care4Animals SMS codes (e.g., DISEASE1–DISEASE10). 🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes Languages: English, Luganda, Runyankole, Acholi, Kiswahili Audience: Farmers, para-vets, village health teams, agriculture st

title: Introduction to Animal Care
content:
Definition: Animal care = daily actions to maintain animal health, comfort, and well-being.
Good care ensures proper nutrition, environment, health, and humane handling.
Media: Intro animation on 'What is Animal Care?'
sms_content: Definition: Animal care = daily actions to maintain animal health, comfort, and well-being. Good care ensures proper nutrition, environment, health, and humane handling. Media: Intro animation on 'What is Animal Care?'

title: The Relationship Between Care and Welfare
content:
Animal care supports welfare by meeting animals’ physical and emotional needs.
Poor care = poor welfare; neglect causes stress and disease.
Activity: Identify welfare issues linked to poor care.
sms_content: Animal care supports welfare by meeting animals’ physical and emotional needs. Poor care = poor welfare; neglect causes stress and disease. Activity: Identify welfare issues linked to poor care.

title: Understanding Animal Needs
content:
Every species and individual animal has unique needs (food, shelter, space, companionship).
Recognize behavioral and physiological signs of unmet needs.
Interactive quiz: Match species to their basic care needs.
sms_content: Every species and individual animal has unique needs (food, shelter, space, companionship). Recognize behavioral and physiological signs of unmet needs. Interactive quiz: Match species to their basic care needs.

title: Nutrition and Feeding
content:
Provide clean water, balanced diet, and regular feeding schedules.
Avoid overfeeding or underfeeding. Adjust diet by species, age, and purpose.
Media: video demo on balanced livestock feeding.
sms_content: Provide clean water, balanced diet, and regular feeding schedules. Avoid overfeeding or underfeeding. Adjust diet by species, age, and purpose. Media: video demo on balanced livestock feeding.

title: Housing and Environment
content:
Shelters must protect from rain, wind, heat, and predators.
Ensure proper ventilation, space, and hygiene.
Infographic: ideal animal housing setup.
sms_content: Shelters must protect from rain, wind, heat, and predators. Ensure proper ventilation, space, and hygiene. Infographic: ideal animal housing setup.

title: Routine Care and Observation
content:
Observe animals daily for changes in behavior, appetite, or injuries.
Routine care includes grooming, hoof trimming, cleaning, and rest.
Checklist: daily animal care routine.
sms_content: Observe animals daily for changes in behavior, appetite, or injuries. Routine care includes grooming, hoof trimming, cleaning, and rest. Checklist: daily animal care routine.

title: Health and Disease Prevention
content:
Keep animals vaccinated, dewormed, and parasite-free.
Isolate sick animals and seek veterinary help early.
Activity: identify signs of common animal diseases.
sms_content: Keep animals vaccinated, dewormed, and parasite-free. Isolate sick animals and seek veterinary help early. Activity: identify signs of common animal diseases.

title: Human–Animal Interaction
content:
Treat animals gently and calmly. Avoid fear or pain during handling.
Positive interaction improves productivity and welfare.
Media: video of calm vs. rough handling impacts.
sms_content: Treat animals gently and calmly. Avoid fear or pain during handling. Positive interaction improves productivity and welfare. Media: video of calm vs. rough handling impacts.

title: Record Keeping in Animal Care
content:
Record feeding, health checks, and breeding to improve management.
Helps monitor productivity and detect issues early.
Template: example daily care log sheet.
sms_content: Record feeding, health checks, and breeding to improve management. Helps monitor productivity and detect issues early. Template: example daily care log sheet.

title: The Role of Animal Health Professionals
content:
Veterinarians and para-vets support farmers with expert guidance.
They promote good care practices, disease prevention, and training.
Media: interview with a local veterinary officer.
💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning)
These SMS lessons summarize the same topics for offline learners. Each message is limited to 480 characters and accessible through the WTS‑LEARN SMS code system (e.g., WTS AC1–AC10).
🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes
Languages: English, Luganda, Runyankole, Kiswahili
• Target Audience: Farmers, para-vets, students, and community animal health workers
• Delivery: WTS‑LEARN app (online) and SMS (offline)
• Partners: Bugema University, MAAIF, and local cooperatives
• Duration: 3–5 minutes (app), 1–2 minutes (SMS)
• Standards: FAO & WOAH animal welfare and care principles
• Objective: Promote humane, effective, and sustainable animal care practices.
sms_content: Veterinarians and para-vets support farmers with expert guidance. They promote good care practices, disease prevention, and training. Media: interview with a local veterinary officer. 💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning) These SMS lessons summarize the same topics for offline learners. Each message is limited to 480 characters and accessible through the WTS‑LEARN SMS code system (e.g., WTS AC1–AC10). 🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes L

title: Introduction to Animal Transport
content:
Definition: Movement of animals from one location to another for trade, slaughter, or breeding.
Objective: Ensure safety, welfare, and minimal stress during movement.
Media: Introductory animation on humane transport importance.
sms_content: Definition: Movement of animals from one location to another for trade, slaughter, or breeding. Objective: Ensure safety, welfare, and minimal stress during movement. Media: Introductory animation on humane transport importance.

title: Principles of Animal Welfare in Transport
content:
Provide adequate space, ventilation, and comfort.
Avoid overcrowding and prolonged travel without rest.
Interactive diagram: animal comfort zones during travel.
sms_content: Provide adequate space, ventilation, and comfort. Avoid overcrowding and prolonged travel without rest. Interactive diagram: animal comfort zones during travel.

title: Preparation Before Transport
content:
Animals should be healthy, fit to travel, and inspected by a veterinarian.
Provide water and rest before loading.
Checklist: pre-transport health inspection steps.
sms_content: Animals should be healthy, fit to travel, and inspected by a veterinarian. Provide water and rest before loading. Checklist: pre-transport health inspection steps.

title: Loading and Unloading Practices
content:
Use ramps with gentle slopes and non-slip surfaces.
Handle animals calmly—no shouting or hitting.
Video demonstration: low-stress loading techniques.
sms_content: Use ramps with gentle slopes and non-slip surfaces. Handle animals calmly—no shouting or hitting. Video demonstration: low-stress loading techniques.

title: Vehicle Design and Equipment
content:
Vehicles must provide adequate ventilation and protection from weather.
Partitions prevent injury and allow separation by species or size.
Infographic: ideal livestock transport truck layout.
sms_content: Vehicles must provide adequate ventilation and protection from weather. Partitions prevent injury and allow separation by species or size. Infographic: ideal livestock transport truck layout.

title: Duration and Rest Periods
content:
Plan journeys to minimize travel time.
Provide water and feed on long trips (>8 hours).
Interactive quiz: rest intervals by animal type.
sms_content: Plan journeys to minimize travel time. Provide water and feed on long trips (>8 hours). Interactive quiz: rest intervals by animal type.

title: Handling During Transit
content:
Avoid harsh driving or sudden stops.
Monitor animals regularly for signs of distress or injury.
Activity: identify unsafe transport behaviors.
sms_content: Avoid harsh driving or sudden stops. Monitor animals regularly for signs of distress or injury. Activity: identify unsafe transport behaviors.

title: Emergencies and Animal Health
content:
Carry first aid kit and emergency contacts for vets.
Stop transport if animals are injured or sick.
Media: emergency response guide animation.
sms_content: Carry first aid kit and emergency contacts for vets. Stop transport if animals are injured or sick. Media: emergency response guide animation.

title: Documentation and Legal Requirements
content:
Transporters must carry movement permits and health certificates.
Comply with national animal welfare laws and veterinary regulations.
Template: sample animal transport permit form.
sms_content: Transporters must carry movement permits and health certificates. Comply with national animal welfare laws and veterinary regulations. Template: sample animal transport permit form.

title: Responsibilities and Ethics
content:
Handlers must act responsibly, prioritizing animal welfare over profit.
Train drivers and workers in humane transport.
Video: case study of good vs. poor transport practices.
💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning)
These SMS lessons summarize the key concepts from the 'Animal Transport' module for farmers and transporters without smartphones. Each message is under 480 characters and accessible through the WTS‑LEARN SMS code system (e.g., WTS TRA1–TRA10).
🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes
Languages: English, Luganda, Runyankole, Kiswahili
• Target Users: Livestock farmers, animal transporters, and veterinary students
• Delivery Channels: WTS‑LEARN app (online) and SMS gateway (offline)
• Partnerships: Bugema University, local veterinary offices, and farmer cooperatives
• Duration: 3–5 minutes per app lesson or 1–2 minutes per SMS message
• Alignment: FAO & OIE animal welfare transport standards
sms_content: Handlers must act responsibly, prioritizing animal welfare over profit. Train drivers and workers in humane transport. Video: case study of good vs. poor transport practices. 💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning) These SMS lessons summarize the key concepts from the 'Animal Transport' module for farmers and transporters without smartphones. Each message is under 480 characters and accessible through the WTS‑LEARN SMS code system (e.g., WTS

title: Understanding Animal Welfare
content:
Welfare = how an animal is coping physically & mentally with its environment.
The Five Freedoms: freedom from hunger/thirst, discomfort, pain/injury, fear, and to express natural behavior.
Good welfare = healthy body, positive mental state, ability to adapt.
Media: Intro video with animal care animations.
Quiz: Name two of the Five Freedoms.
sms_content: Welfare = how an animal is coping physically & mentally with its environment. The Five Freedoms: freedom from hunger/thirst, discomfort, pain/injury, fear, and to express natural behavior. Good welfare = healthy body, positive mental state, ability to adapt. Media: Intro video with animal care animations. Quiz: Name two of the Five Freedoms.

title: The Five Domains Model
content:
Nutrition, Environment, Health, Behavior, Mental State – the holistic approach to welfare.
Activity: Tap each domain to see real examples.
Reflection: How can good feeding improve mental state?
sms_content: Nutrition, Environment, Health, Behavior, Mental State – the holistic approach to welfare. Activity: Tap each domain to see real examples. Reflection: How can good feeding improve mental state?

title: Welfare Indicators
content:
Types: Animal-based (body condition, injuries), Resource-based (space, water, shelter), Management-based (handling).
Infographic shows examples in cattle, goats, pigs.
sms_content: Types: Animal-based (body condition, injuries), Resource-based (space, water, shelter), Management-based (handling). Infographic shows examples in cattle, goats, pigs.

title: Welfare Assessment Tools
content:
Welfare Quality®, WSAVA, OIE guidelines.
Interactive icons explain each tool.
Quiz: Which tool is for companion animals?
sms_content: Welfare Quality®, WSAVA, OIE guidelines. Interactive icons explain each tool. Quiz: Which tool is for companion animals?

title: Steps in Conducting a Welfare Assessment
content:
1 Prepare (select animals & indicators).
2 Observe environment.
3 Score indicators.
4 Record & compare.
5 Give feedback.
Media: Animated farm assessment.
sms_content: 1 Prepare (select animals & indicators). 2 Observe environment. 3 Score indicators. 4 Record & compare. 5 Give feedback. Media: Animated farm assessment.

title: Interpreting Welfare Scores
content:
Scoring scale 0 = good → 2 = poor.
Summarize results under feeding, housing, health, behavior.
Activity: Practice scoring example animals.
sms_content: Scoring scale 0 = good → 2 = poor. Summarize results under feeding, housing, health, behavior. Activity: Practice scoring example animals.

title: Ethics & Human–Animal Interaction
content:
Compassionate care reduces stress & improves performance.
Recognize animal emotions and treat kindly.
Video: Farmer–animal trust story.
sms_content: Compassionate care reduces stress & improves performance. Recognize animal emotions and treat kindly. Video: Farmer–animal trust story.

title: Reporting & Continuous Improvement
content:
Share results & plan improvements.
Monitor changes after implementation.
Upload sample welfare report.
Quiz: Why monitor progress?
💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning)
Each microlesson fits in 3–4 SMS messages (≤480 characters). Farmers access by texting codes (e.g., WTS AWARE1–AWARE9).
🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes
Languages: English, Luganda, Runyankole, Kiswahili.
• Cultural Fit: Examples from smallholder systems (cattle, goats, pigs, poultry).
• Integration: Farmer groups & radio broadcast.
• Offline Access: SMS keywords like 'WTS AWARE5' for lesson retrieval.
• Ethical Oversight: Bugema University ensures humane communication & consent.
sms_content: Share results & plan improvements. Monitor changes after implementation. Upload sample welfare report. Quiz: Why monitor progress? 💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning) Each microlesson fits in 3–4 SMS messages (≤480 characters). Farmers access by texting codes (e.g., WTS AWARE1–AWARE9). 🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes Languages: English, Luganda, Runyankole, Kiswahili. • Cultural Fit: Examples from smallholder systems (cattle, goats,

title: What Is Animal Welfare Legislation?
content:
Animal welfare legislation defines the rights and protection animals receive under law.
It ensures humane treatment, prevents cruelty, and promotes responsible ownership.
Media: Intro video with farmer-vet discussions.
Quiz: Why is animal welfare law important?
sms_content: Animal welfare legislation defines the rights and protection animals receive under law. It ensures humane treatment, prevents cruelty, and promotes responsible ownership. Media: Intro video with farmer-vet discussions. Quiz: Why is animal welfare law important?

title: International Legal Frameworks
content:
Global organizations: OIE/WOAH, FAO, and the Universal Declaration on Animal Welfare.
These set standards for humane treatment and trade in animals.
Interactive map: Click regions to view key laws.
Reflection: How do global standards influence national laws?
sms_content: Global organizations: OIE/WOAH, FAO, and the Universal Declaration on Animal Welfare. These set standards for humane treatment and trade in animals. Interactive map: Click regions to view key laws. Reflection: How do global standards influence national laws?

title: African & Regional Frameworks
content:
African Union (AU-IBAR) promotes animal welfare in livestock development.
Regional trade laws require humane transport and slaughter.
Case example: East African Community standards for animal transport.
sms_content: African Union (AU-IBAR) promotes animal welfare in livestock development. Regional trade laws require humane transport and slaughter. Case example: East African Community standards for animal transport.

title: National Legislation (Uganda Example)
content:
Uganda Animal Diseases Act (Cap. 38): Prevents cruelty, regulates transport, slaughter, and treatment.
Animal Welfare Bill: Emphasizes humane care and penalties for neglect.
Media: Infographic of farmer responsibilities.
Quiz: What are key legal duties of animal owners?
sms_content: Uganda Animal Diseases Act (Cap. 38): Prevents cruelty, regulates transport, slaughter, and treatment. Animal Welfare Bill: Emphasizes humane care and penalties for neglect. Media: Infographic of farmer responsibilities. Quiz: What are key legal duties of animal owners?

title: Enforcement & Compliance
content:
Who enforces? Veterinary officers, police, and courts.
Inspections ensure compliance with welfare and disease laws.
Activity: Drag and drop roles of each authority.
Quiz: Who should report animal abuse?
sms_content: Who enforces? Veterinary officers, police, and courts. Inspections ensure compliance with welfare and disease laws. Activity: Drag and drop roles of each authority. Quiz: Who should report animal abuse?

title: Penalties & Legal Consequences
content:
Acts of cruelty attract fines or imprisonment.
Examples: Overloading, starvation, and beating animals.
Media: Story-based simulation – identify violations.
Quiz: What is the penalty for animal cruelty?
sms_content: Acts of cruelty attract fines or imprisonment. Examples: Overloading, starvation, and beating animals. Media: Story-based simulation – identify violations. Quiz: What is the penalty for animal cruelty?

title: Farmer Rights and Responsibilities
content:
Farmers must provide food, water, shelter, and medical care.
Animals are recognized as sentient beings deserving care.
Task: Interactive checklist of daily welfare duties.
Quiz: Which actions show compliance?
sms_content: Farmers must provide food, water, shelter, and medical care. Animals are recognized as sentient beings deserving care. Task: Interactive checklist of daily welfare duties. Quiz: Which actions show compliance?

title: Ethical Principles and Community Action
content:
Ethics complement laws by promoting compassion and social responsibility.
Community watch groups can help report and prevent cruelty.
Media: Roleplay of community awareness event.
sms_content: Ethics complement laws by promoting compassion and social responsibility. Community watch groups can help report and prevent cruelty. Media: Roleplay of community awareness event.

title: Continuous Education & Advocacy
content:
Stay informed through extension services and local government.
Participate in awareness campaigns to strengthen welfare culture.
Activity: Upload your community welfare pledge.
💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning)
Each SMS lesson fits within 3–4 messages (≤480 characters). Farmers can text lesson codes (e.g., WTS LAW1–LAW9) to receive the content.
🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes
Languages: English, Luganda, Kiswahili, Runyankole.
• Regional Fit: Reflects Ugandan and East African legal frameworks.
• Integration: Farmer radio programs, school outreach, and community extension.
• Offline Access: Farmers text lesson codes (e.g., 'WTS LAW5') to receive content.
• Oversight: Bugema University ensures ethical standards and legal accuracy.
sms_content: Stay informed through extension services and local government. Participate in awareness campaigns to strengthen welfare culture. Activity: Upload your community welfare pledge. 💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning) Each SMS lesson fits within 3–4 messages (≤480 characters). Farmers can text lesson codes (e.g., WTS LAW1–LAW9) to receive the content. 🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes Languages: English, Luganda, Kiswahili, Runyankole. • R

title: What Is a Clinical Examination?
content:
Understand the meaning and purpose of clinical examination.
Definition: Evaluation of an animal’s general state and physiological parameters.
Purpose: Assess health, welfare, diagnose disease, and monitor therapy.
Interactive Task: Watch explainer animation showing a vet examining cattle.
Quiz: Why is a clinical examination important for animal welfare?
sms_content: Understand the meaning and purpose of clinical examination. Definition: Evaluation of an animal’s general state and physiological parameters. Purpose: Assess health, welfare, diagnose disease, and monitor therapy. Interactive Task: Watch explainer animation showing a vet examining cattle. Quiz: Why is a clinical examination important for animal welfare?

title: Roles and Responsibilities
content:
Differentiate between the roles of animal keepers and professionals.
Animal keepers detect abnormalities; animal health professionals perform full exams.
Stress from poor handling affects results and welfare.
Task: Identify examples of animal stressors.
Quiz: Who should perform a full clinical examination?
sms_content: Differentiate between the roles of animal keepers and professionals. Animal keepers detect abnormalities; animal health professionals perform full exams. Stress from poor handling affects results and welfare. Task: Identify examples of animal stressors. Quiz: Who should perform a full clinical examination?

title: Safety and Handling
content:
Identify safety practices during an examination.
Observe animal behavior before touching.
Use verbal, physical, or chemical restraint as appropriate.
Calm handling prevents injuries and improves accuracy.
Video: Safe approach techniques.
sms_content: Identify safety practices during an examination. Observe animal behavior before touching. Use verbal, physical, or chemical restraint as appropriate. Calm handling prevents injuries and improves accuracy. Video: Safe approach techniques.

title: Tools of the Trade
content:
Recognize essential veterinary equipment.
Basic tools: stethoscope, thermometer, watch, gloves.
Advanced tools: camera, syringes, sample containers.
Most important tool: your senses.
Interactive labeling of tools and their functions.
sms_content: Recognize essential veterinary equipment. Basic tools: stethoscope, thermometer, watch, gloves. Advanced tools: camera, syringes, sample containers. Most important tool: your senses. Interactive labeling of tools and their functions.

title: Step-by-Step Examination
content:
Learn the systematic procedure.
1. Signalment  2. Medical History  3. Physical Exam  4. Tests  5. Diagnosis  6. Treatment.
Interactive flow diagram with step explanations.
Quiz: Which step comes first?
sms_content: Learn the systematic procedure. 1. Signalment 2. Medical History 3. Physical Exam 4. Tests 5. Diagnosis 6. Treatment. Interactive flow diagram with step explanations. Quiz: Which step comes first?

title: Recognizing Vital Signs
content:
Know how to measure respiration, heart rate, perfusion, hydration, and temperature.
Learn normal parameters for each species.
Understand signs of dehydration and stress.
Infographic chart of normal vital signs.
Activity: Measure respiration (15 s × 4 rule).
sms_content: Know how to measure respiration, heart rate, perfusion, hydration, and temperature. Learn normal parameters for each species. Understand signs of dehydration and stress. Infographic chart of normal vital signs. Activity: Measure respiration (15 s × 4 rule).

title: Physical Examination
content:
Apply the 'Hands-off to Hands-on' approach.
Observe posture and coat before touching.
Palpate, percuss, and auscultate step-by-step.
Video: Demonstration of stethoscope placement.
Quiz: What should you check before touching an animal?
sms_content: Apply the 'Hands-off to Hands-on' approach. Observe posture and coat before touching. Palpate, percuss, and auscultate step-by-step. Video: Demonstration of stethoscope placement. Quiz: What should you check before touching an animal?

title: Diagnosis and Treatment
content:
Understand the flow from observation to treatment.
List possible causes = differential diagnosis.
Use tests to confirm findings.
Follow up and monitor recovery.
Quiz: What is the goal of a differential diagnosis?
sms_content: Understand the flow from observation to treatment. List possible causes = differential diagnosis. Use tests to confirm findings. Follow up and monitor recovery. Quiz: What is the goal of a differential diagnosis?

title: Review and Field Practice
content:
Reinforce learning through real cases.
Apply in farmer groups with supervision.
Record examination results using provided forms.
Final self-assessment and summary.
💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning)
Each microlesson fits within 3–4 SMS messages (≤480 characters total). Farmers reply with short codes to proceed to the next lesson.
🌍 Localization & Delivery Notes
Languages: English, Luganda, Kiswahili, Runyankole (for pilot translation).
• Cultural Context: Examples include cattle, goats, and dogs common in East Africa.
• Integration: Use with radio broadcasts and farmer groups.
• Offline Access: Farmers can text lesson codes (e.g., WTS CLINEXM5) to receive SMS content.
• Monitoring: Track progress through app analytics and SMS code responses.
sms_content: Reinforce learning through real cases. Apply in farmer groups with supervision. Record examination results using provided forms. Final self-assessment and summary. 💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning) Each microlesson fits within 3–4 SMS messages (≤480 characters total). Farmers reply with short codes to proceed to the next lesson. 🌍 Localization & Delivery Notes Languages: English, Luganda, Kiswahili, Runyankole (for pilot translation). •

title: Understanding Euthanasia
content:
Definition: Humane termination of an animal’s life to relieve incurable pain or suffering.
Goal: Minimize pain, distress, and anxiety before loss of consciousness.
Media: Introductory animation showing euthanasia as a welfare measure.
Quiz: What does humane euthanasia mean?
sms_content: Definition: Humane termination of an animal’s life to relieve incurable pain or suffering. Goal: Minimize pain, distress, and anxiety before loss of consciousness. Media: Introductory animation showing euthanasia as a welfare measure. Quiz: What does humane euthanasia mean?

title: Ethical Principles in Euthanasia
content:
Euthanasia decisions must balance animal welfare, human safety, and compassion.
Consider suffering, prognosis, and quality of life.
Interactive reflection: Discuss when euthanasia becomes ethically justified.
sms_content: Euthanasia decisions must balance animal welfare, human safety, and compassion. Consider suffering, prognosis, and quality of life. Interactive reflection: Discuss when euthanasia becomes ethically justified.

title: Decision-Making Criteria
content:
Euthanasia should be considered if pain is unmanageable, disease incurable, or suffering severe.
Veterinary assessment and owner consent are mandatory.
Quiz: List two valid reasons for euthanasia.
sms_content: Euthanasia should be considered if pain is unmanageable, disease incurable, or suffering severe. Veterinary assessment and owner consent are mandatory. Quiz: List two valid reasons for euthanasia.

title: Animal Welfare Considerations
content:
Ensure minimal handling stress, quiet environment, and familiar caretaker presence.
Animals should not witness euthanasia of others.
Media: Video demonstrating low-stress animal handling before euthanasia.
sms_content: Ensure minimal handling stress, quiet environment, and familiar caretaker presence. Animals should not witness euthanasia of others. Media: Video demonstrating low-stress animal handling before euthanasia.

title: Euthanasia Methods Overview
content:
Methods: Chemical (injectable agents) and physical (gunshot, captive bolt).
Choice depends on species, size, temperament, and available resources.
Activity: Match species to suitable method.
sms_content: Methods: Chemical (injectable agents) and physical (gunshot, captive bolt). Choice depends on species, size, temperament, and available resources. Activity: Match species to suitable method.

title: Chemical Methods
content:
Common agents: barbiturates, anaesthetic overdoses, potassium chloride (under anaesthesia).
Advantages: rapid unconsciousness, minimal pain, reliability.
Quiz: What is the preferred chemical for euthanasia in small animals?
sms_content: Common agents: barbiturates, anaesthetic overdoses, potassium chloride (under anaesthesia). Advantages: rapid unconsciousness, minimal pain, reliability. Quiz: What is the preferred chemical for euthanasia in small animals?

title: Physical Methods
content:
Captive bolt or firearm for large livestock and wildlife.
Require trained personnel, correct placement, and safety precautions.
Interactive visual: target placement diagram for cattle and goats.
sms_content: Captive bolt or firearm for large livestock and wildlife. Require trained personnel, correct placement, and safety precautions. Interactive visual: target placement diagram for cattle and goats.

title: Confirmation of Death
content:
Check absence of heartbeat, respiration, and reflexes before disposal.
Record time and method of euthanasia.
Quiz: Name two signs confirming death.
sms_content: Check absence of heartbeat, respiration, and reflexes before disposal. Record time and method of euthanasia. Quiz: Name two signs confirming death.

title: Disposal and Biosecurity
content:
Carcass disposal: burial, incineration, rendering per regulations.
Avoid environmental contamination and disease spread.
Infographic: safe carcass disposal flowchart.
sms_content: Carcass disposal: burial, incineration, rendering per regulations. Avoid environmental contamination and disease spread. Infographic: safe carcass disposal flowchart.

title: Emotional Support and Debriefing
content:
Recognize emotional impact on handlers and owners.
Offer counseling or peer support after euthanasia procedures.
Media: short testimonial from a vet on compassion fatigue.
Quiz: Why is emotional support important post-euthanasia?
💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning)
These SMS lessons summarize each app-based topic in short, easy-to-understand messages. Each message fits within 480 characters and is accessible by texting codes (e.g., WTS EUT1–EUT10).
🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes
Languages: English, Luganda, Runyankole, Kiswahili
• Target Audience: Farmers, para-vets, and animal welfare students
• Delivery: WTS‑LEARN app (online) and SMS gateway (offline)
• Context: Designed for smallholder farmers & veterinary training under Bugema University ethics
• Focus: Humane, compassionate, and lawful handling of euthanasia in East Africa.
sms_content: Recognize emotional impact on handlers and owners. Offer counseling or peer support after euthanasia procedures. Media: short testimonial from a vet on compassion fatigue. Quiz: Why is emotional support important post-euthanasia? 💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning) These SMS lessons summarize each app-based topic in short, easy-to-understand messages. Each message fits within 480 characters and is accessible by texting codes (e.g., WTS EU

title: Understanding Fish Welfare
content:
Definition: Fish welfare = physical and mental well-being of fish in farming or capture systems.
Key principle: Treat fish as sentient beings capable of feeling pain and stress.
Media: Animation explaining fish perception and welfare importance.
sms_content: Definition: Fish welfare = physical and mental well-being of fish in farming or capture systems. Key principle: Treat fish as sentient beings capable of feeling pain and stress. Media: Animation explaining fish perception and welfare importance.

title: The Five Freedoms of Fish Welfare
content:
Freedom from hunger/thirst, discomfort, pain/injury, fear/distress, and to express natural behavior.
Interactive checklist: assess each freedom in a fishpond context.
sms_content: Freedom from hunger/thirst, discomfort, pain/injury, fear/distress, and to express natural behavior. Interactive checklist: assess each freedom in a fishpond context.

title: Water Quality and Environment
content:
Water parameters (temperature, oxygen, pH, ammonia) directly affect welfare.
Good water = healthy fish = better productivity.
Diagram: ideal water conditions for tilapia and catfish.
sms_content: Water parameters (temperature, oxygen, pH, ammonia) directly affect welfare. Good water = healthy fish = better productivity. Diagram: ideal water conditions for tilapia and catfish.

title: Stocking Density
content:
Overcrowding increases stress, disease, and mortality.
Follow species-specific density guidelines.
Media: video comparing overcrowded vs. optimal fish tanks.
sms_content: Overcrowding increases stress, disease, and mortality. Follow species-specific density guidelines. Media: video comparing overcrowded vs. optimal fish tanks.

title: Feeding and Nutrition
content:
Provide balanced diet, proper feeding times, and clean feed.
Avoid overfeeding to prevent water pollution.
Quiz: how feeding behavior indicates fish stress.
sms_content: Provide balanced diet, proper feeding times, and clean feed. Avoid overfeeding to prevent water pollution. Quiz: how feeding behavior indicates fish stress.

title: Handling and Transport
content:
Use soft nets; avoid rough handling and temperature shock.
Reduce transport duration and maintain oxygen levels.
Media: demonstration of safe fish handling.
sms_content: Use soft nets; avoid rough handling and temperature shock. Reduce transport duration and maintain oxygen levels. Media: demonstration of safe fish handling.

title: Disease Prevention and Health Monitoring
content:
Observe behavior: appetite, swimming, and gill color indicate health.
Implement biosecurity measures and isolate sick fish.
Infographic: daily fish health checklist.
sms_content: Observe behavior: appetite, swimming, and gill color indicate health. Implement biosecurity measures and isolate sick fish. Infographic: daily fish health checklist.

title: Humane Harvesting and Slaughter
content:
Fish should be stunned before killing to prevent pain.
Avoid asphyxiation or freezing of live fish.
Video: humane slaughter methods for aquaculture.
sms_content: Fish should be stunned before killing to prevent pain. Avoid asphyxiation or freezing of live fish. Video: humane slaughter methods for aquaculture.

title: Record Keeping and Traceability
content:
Keep records of feed, health, and mortality for farm management and compliance.
Template: daily welfare record sheet example.
sms_content: Keep records of feed, health, and mortality for farm management and compliance. Template: daily welfare record sheet example.

title: Ethics and Sustainability
content:
Fish welfare supports environmental sustainability and food safety.
Respect local ecosystems and avoid pollution.
Interactive reflection: why welfare = better aquaculture.
💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning)
These SMS lessons summarize the app-based topics into short, simple texts for rural farmers or learners without smartphones. Each message is under 480 characters and accessible by texting WTS FW1–FW10.
🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes
Languages: English, Luganda, Runyankole, Kiswahili
• Target Audience: Fish farmers, aquaculture extension officers, and students
• Delivery: WTS‑LEARN mobile app (online) and SMS gateway (offline)
• Partners: Bugema University, fisheries departments, local cooperatives
• Duration: 3–5 minutes (app), 1–2 minutes (SMS)
• Standards: FAO, WOAH, and Uganda’s fisheries welfare guidelines
• Outcome: Promote humane, ethical, and sustainable aquaculture practices.
sms_content: Fish welfare supports environmental sustainability and food safety. Respect local ecosystems and avoid pollution. Interactive reflection: why welfare = better aquaculture. 💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning) These SMS lessons summarize the app-based topics into short, simple texts for rural farmers or learners without smartphones. Each message is under 480 characters and accessible by texting WTS FW1–FW10. 🌍 Localization & Implementation

title: Introduction to Humane Slaughter
content:
Definition: Humane slaughter ensures animals are killed with minimal pain, fear, or distress.
Goal: Welfare, food quality, and ethical responsibility.
Media: Animated overview of humane slaughter process.
sms_content: Definition: Humane slaughter ensures animals are killed with minimal pain, fear, or distress. Goal: Welfare, food quality, and ethical responsibility. Media: Animated overview of humane slaughter process.

title: Animal Welfare and Ethics
content:
Welfare = physical and psychological well-being before, during, and after slaughter.
Animals should be calm, comfortable, and free from unnecessary suffering.
Activity: Identify welfare breaches in sample abattoir scenarios.
sms_content: Welfare = physical and psychological well-being before, during, and after slaughter. Animals should be calm, comfortable, and free from unnecessary suffering. Activity: Identify welfare breaches in sample abattoir scenarios.

title: Pre-Slaughter Handling
content:
Handle animals calmly; avoid shouting or striking.
Provide water, avoid overcrowding, and rest animals before slaughter.
Media: Video on low-stress animal handling techniques.
sms_content: Handle animals calmly; avoid shouting or striking. Provide water, avoid overcrowding, and rest animals before slaughter. Media: Video on low-stress animal handling techniques.

title: Facilities and Equipment
content:
Use properly designed pens, races, and stunning areas.
Equipment should be well-maintained and used by trained personnel.
Infographic: design of an ideal slaughter facility.
sms_content: Use properly designed pens, races, and stunning areas. Equipment should be well-maintained and used by trained personnel. Infographic: design of an ideal slaughter facility.

title: Stunning Methods Overview
content:
Purpose: Render animal unconscious before killing to prevent pain.
Methods: captive bolt, electrical, and CO₂ stunning (species-dependent).
Interactive quiz: match stunning methods with animal types.
sms_content: Purpose: Render animal unconscious before killing to prevent pain. Methods: captive bolt, electrical, and CO₂ stunning (species-dependent). Interactive quiz: match stunning methods with animal types.

title: Captive Bolt Stunning
content:
Used for cattle, sheep, and goats.
Place bolt correctly on the skull; ensure immediate collapse.
Media: diagram of proper bolt placement.
sms_content: Used for cattle, sheep, and goats. Place bolt correctly on the skull; ensure immediate collapse. Media: diagram of proper bolt placement.

title: Electrical Stunning
content:
Used for pigs and poultry.
Apply correct voltage and duration; avoid repeated shocks.
Interactive activity: check parameters for species-specific stunning.
sms_content: Used for pigs and poultry. Apply correct voltage and duration; avoid repeated shocks. Interactive activity: check parameters for species-specific stunning.

title: Bleeding and Exsanguination
content:
Bleeding must be rapid to ensure death without recovery from stunning.
Cut major blood vessels in the neck or chest immediately after stunning.
Video: demonstration of safe and quick bleeding procedures.
sms_content: Bleeding must be rapid to ensure death without recovery from stunning. Cut major blood vessels in the neck or chest immediately after stunning. Video: demonstration of safe and quick bleeding procedures.

title: Post-Slaughter Verification
content:
Confirm death: no breathing, reflexes, or blinking.
Do not begin dressing until death is verified.
Checklist: indicators confirming death post-stunning.
sms_content: Confirm death: no breathing, reflexes, or blinking. Do not begin dressing until death is verified. Checklist: indicators confirming death post-stunning.

title: Worker Training and Ethics
content:
All slaughter personnel should be trained and certified.
Maintain respect, professionalism, and compassion at all times.
Media: testimonial video from a humane slaughter trainer.
💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning)
These SMS lessons summarize the humane slaughter principles for quick access by farmers, butchers, and transporters. Each message is ≤480 characters and available via codes (e.g., WTS HS1–HS10).
🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes
Languages: English, Luganda, Runyankole, Kiswahili
• Target Audience: Farmers, butchers, abattoir workers, and vet trainees
• Delivery: WTS‑LEARN app and SMS gateway (offline)
• Partnerships: Bugema University, Ministry of Agriculture, and local abattoirs
• Duration: 3–5 minutes (app), 1–2 minutes (SMS)
• Compliance: OIE, FAO, and national animal welfare laws
• Objective: Reduce suffering, improve welfare, ensure safe, ethical meat production.
sms_content: All slaughter personnel should be trained and certified. Maintain respect, professionalism, and compassion at all times. Media: testimonial video from a humane slaughter trainer. 💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning) These SMS lessons summarize the humane slaughter principles for quick access by farmers, butchers, and transporters. Each message is ≤480 characters and available via codes (e.g., WTS HS1–HS10). 🌍 Localization & Implementation

title: Understanding Animal Welfare
content:
Definition: Animal welfare = the physical and psychological state of an animal in relation to its environment.
Goal: 3
Media: Introductory animation explaining animal welfare concepts.
sms_content: Definition: Animal welfare = the physical and psychological state of an animal in relation to its environment. Goal: 3 Media: Introductory animation explaining animal welfare concepts.

title: Importance of Animal Welfare
content:
Good welfare benefits animals, farmers, consumers, and the environment.
Healthy, well-treated animals are more productive and resilient.
Activity: Match welfare benefits to examples (milk yield, reproduction, growth).
sms_content: Good welfare benefits animals, farmers, consumers, and the environment. Healthy, well-treated animals are more productive and resilient. Activity: Match welfare benefits to examples (milk yield, reproduction, growth).

title: Animal Sentience and Emotions
content:
Animals can feel pain, fear, and pleasure—they are sentient beings.
Respecting sentience is the foundation of welfare.
Media: video showing positive and negative animal behaviors.
sms_content: Animals can feel pain, fear, and pleasure—they are sentient beings. Respecting sentience is the foundation of welfare. Media: video showing positive and negative animal behaviors.

title: The Five Freedoms Framework
content:
1) Freedom from hunger/thirst
2) Freedom from discomfort
3) Freedom from pain/injury
4) Freedom from fear/distress
5) Freedom to express normal behavior.
Interactive quiz: identify which freedom is violated in sample scenarios.
sms_content: 1) Freedom from hunger/thirst 2) Freedom from discomfort 3) Freedom from pain/injury 4) Freedom from fear/distress 5) Freedom to express normal behavior. Interactive quiz: identify which freedom is violated in sample scenarios.

title: The Five Domains Model
content:
Expands the Five Freedoms to include mental states (nutrition, environment, health, behavior, and mental experience).
Helps assess both positive and negative welfare.
Infographic: The Five Domains wheel diagram.
sms_content: Expands the Five Freedoms to include mental states (nutrition, environment, health, behavior, and mental experience). Helps assess both positive and negative welfare. Infographic: The Five Domains wheel diagram.

title: Human–Animal Relationship
content:
Human handling and care affect welfare outcomes.
Positive interaction reduces fear and increases productivity.
Media: demonstration of gentle vs. rough handling impacts.
sms_content: Human handling and care affect welfare outcomes. Positive interaction reduces fear and increases productivity. Media: demonstration of gentle vs. rough handling impacts.

title: Animal Welfare and Ethics
content:
Ethics: treating animals with compassion and respect as living beings.
Moral responsibility of humans to minimize harm and promote well-being.
Reflection: Discuss why animal welfare is a moral duty.
sms_content: Ethics: treating animals with compassion and respect as living beings. Moral responsibility of humans to minimize harm and promote well-being. Reflection: Discuss why animal welfare is a moral duty.

title: Welfare Assessment Indicators
content:
Use behavioral, physiological, and environmental indicators to assess welfare.
Example: posture, vocalization, feeding habits, injury signs.
Checklist: sample welfare observation form.
sms_content: Use behavioral, physiological, and environmental indicators to assess welfare. Example: posture, vocalization, feeding habits, injury signs. Checklist: sample welfare observation form.

title: The Role of Animal Health Professionals
content:
Vets and para-vets are key advocates for welfare—ensure humane care, treatment, and prevention.
Encourage education, policy support, and farmer training.
Media: interview with vet on promoting welfare in practice.
sms_content: Vets and para-vets are key advocates for welfare—ensure humane care, treatment, and prevention. Encourage education, policy support, and farmer training. Media: interview with vet on promoting welfare in practice.

title: Global and Local Welfare Standards
content:
Refer to OIE/WOAH guidelines, FAO frameworks, and national welfare acts.
Local adaptation ensures relevance to cultural and economic contexts.
Activity: identify your country’s animal welfare law or policy.
💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning)
These SMS lessons summarize key concepts in simple language for offline learners. Each message is under 480 characters and is accessed via codes WTS AW1–AW10.
🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes
Languages: English, Luganda, Runyankole, Kiswahili
• Target Audience: Farmers, students, para-vets, and community leaders
• Delivery: WTS‑LEARN mobile app (online) and SMS gateway (offline)
• Duration: 3–5 min (app), 1–2 min (SMS)
• Partners: Bugema University, Ministry of Agriculture, Animal Industry & Fisheries
• Goal: Foster humane treatment, productivity, and compassion toward all animals.
sms_content: Refer to OIE/WOAH guidelines, FAO frameworks, and national welfare acts. Local adaptation ensures relevance to cultural and economic contexts. Activity: identify your country’s animal welfare law or policy. 💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning) These SMS lessons summarize key concepts in simple language for offline learners. Each message is under 480 characters and is accessed via codes WTS AW1–AW10. 🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes La

title: Understanding Animal Pain
content:
Pain is a sensory and emotional experience related to tissue damage.
Animals experience pain similarly to humans; lack of visible signs doesn’t mean absence of pain.
Adaptive vs. maladaptive pain: short-term protective vs. chronic harmful.
Media: Animated demonstration of animal reactions to pain stimuli.
Quiz: Which type of pain has no healing purpose?
sms_content: Pain is a sensory and emotional experience related to tissue damage. Animals experience pain similarly to humans; lack of visible signs doesn’t mean absence of pain. Adaptive vs. maladaptive pain: short-term protective vs. chronic harmful. Media: Animated demonstration of animal reactions to pain stimuli. Quiz: Which type of pain has no healing purpose?

title: Welfare and Pain Relationship
content:
Pain lowers animal welfare and productivity.
Good welfare helps reduce perceived pain (positive mental state).
Media: Chart linking pain to welfare outcomes (healing speed, productivity).
Quiz: How does stress affect pain perception?
sms_content: Pain lowers animal welfare and productivity. Good welfare helps reduce perceived pain (positive mental state). Media: Chart linking pain to welfare outcomes (healing speed, productivity). Quiz: How does stress affect pain perception?

title: Basics of Pain Physiology
content:
Nociceptors detect harmful stimuli; pain perception occurs in the brain.
Stress can either dull (SIA) or amplify (SIH) pain sensation.
Interactive diagram of the pain pathway: stimulus → transmission → perception.
Quiz: What is the difference between nociception and pain perception?
sms_content: Nociceptors detect harmful stimuli; pain perception occurs in the brain. Stress can either dull (SIA) or amplify (SIH) pain sensation. Interactive diagram of the pain pathway: stimulus → transmission → perception. Quiz: What is the difference between nociception and pain perception?

title: Detecting Pain in Animals
content:
Pain signs vary by species, breed, and temperament.
Behavioural signs: restlessness, aggression, isolation, loss of appetite.
Physiological signs: increased heart rate, respiration, temperature.
Media: Comparison of pain expressions across species.
Quiz: What are three physiological indicators of pain?
sms_content: Pain signs vary by species, breed, and temperament. Behavioural signs: restlessness, aggression, isolation, loss of appetite. Physiological signs: increased heart rate, respiration, temperature. Media: Comparison of pain expressions across species. Quiz: What are three physiological indicators of pain?

title: Pain Assessment Tools
content:
Use pain scoring systems to evaluate severity.
Simple descriptive, numerical, and facial expression scales are common tools.
Activity: Match the facial cues with pain intensity levels.
Quiz: What is the advantage of facial expression scales?
sms_content: Use pain scoring systems to evaluate severity. Simple descriptive, numerical, and facial expression scales are common tools. Activity: Match the facial cues with pain intensity levels. Quiz: What is the advantage of facial expression scales?

title: Pain Alleviation Strategies
content:
Pain management must be individual, integrative, and pre-emptive.
Methods: pharmacological, non-pharmacological, care and environment.
Video: Demonstration of integrative pain management in cattle.
Quiz: Why is multimodal pain management recommended?
sms_content: Pain management must be individual, integrative, and pre-emptive. Methods: pharmacological, non-pharmacological, care and environment. Video: Demonstration of integrative pain management in cattle. Quiz: Why is multimodal pain management recommended?

title: Pharmacological Approaches
content:
Mild to strong analgesics: NSAIDs, corticosteroids, opioids, ketamine.
Use appropriate drug combination (multimodal therapy).
Interactive: Build a treatment plan for acute vs. chronic pain.
Quiz: Which drug group provides anti-inflammatory benefits?
sms_content: Mild to strong analgesics: NSAIDs, corticosteroids, opioids, ketamine. Use appropriate drug combination (multimodal therapy). Interactive: Build a treatment plan for acute vs. chronic pain. Quiz: Which drug group provides anti-inflammatory benefits?

title: Non-Drug Pain Relief
content:
Methods: heat/cold therapy, physiotherapy, acupuncture, stress reduction.
Good housing and calm handling enhance recovery.
Media: Video on alternative care methods.
Quiz: List two non-pharmacological methods for pain relief.
sms_content: Methods: heat/cold therapy, physiotherapy, acupuncture, stress reduction. Good housing and calm handling enhance recovery. Media: Video on alternative care methods. Quiz: List two non-pharmacological methods for pain relief.

title: Painful Routine Procedures
content:
Examples: castration, dehorning, tail docking, branding.
Apply the 3S approach: Suppress, Substitute, Soothe.
Interactive scenario: Plan pain control for dehorning.
Quiz: What does the 3S approach stand for?
sms_content: Examples: castration, dehorning, tail docking, branding. Apply the 3S approach: Suppress, Substitute, Soothe. Interactive scenario: Plan pain control for dehorning. Quiz: What does the 3S approach stand for?

title: Role of Animal Health Professionals
content:
Vets should detect, prevent, and treat pain proactively.
Educate animal keepers on pain signs and prevention.
Encourage humane practices and research in pain management.
Quiz: Why is early pain detection critical to welfare?
💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning)
Farmers can access these brief lessons via SMS by texting codes (e.g., WTS PM1–PM10). Each message is under 480 characters and available in English, Luganda, Runyankole, and Kiswahili.
🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes
Languages: English, Luganda, Runyankole, Kiswahili
• Focus: Livestock farmers and para-vets in Uganda & East Africa
• Access: via WTS‑LEARN app or SMS gateway (offline mode)
• Oversight: Bugema University ensures ethics, translation quality, and farmer engagement
• Dissemination: Radio talk shows, vet outreach, farmer group discussions.
sms_content: Vets should detect, prevent, and treat pain proactively. Educate animal keepers on pain signs and prevention. Encourage humane practices and research in pain management. Quiz: Why is early pain detection critical to welfare? 💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning) Farmers can access these brief lessons via SMS by texting codes (e.g., WTS PM1–PM10). Each message is under 480 characters and available in English, Luganda, Runyankole, and Kiswahi

title: Introduction to Antimicrobials
content:
Antimicrobials are medicines that kill or inhibit bacteria, viruses, fungi, and parasites.
Examples: antibiotics, antivirals, antifungals, antiparasitics.
Video: Overview of how antimicrobials work.
Quiz: What is the difference between antibiotics and antimicrobials?
sms_content: Antimicrobials are medicines that kill or inhibit bacteria, viruses, fungi, and parasites. Examples: antibiotics, antivirals, antifungals, antiparasitics. Video: Overview of how antimicrobials work. Quiz: What is the difference between antibiotics and antimicrobials?

title: Why Responsible Use Matters
content:
Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) occurs when pathogens adapt and stop responding to drugs.
AMR leads to treatment failure and disease spread.
Interactive graph: trends in AMR cases globally.
Reflection: How does misuse of antibiotics affect animal welfare?
sms_content: Antimicrobial resistance (AMR) occurs when pathogens adapt and stop responding to drugs. AMR leads to treatment failure and disease spread. Interactive graph: trends in AMR cases globally. Reflection: How does misuse of antibiotics affect animal welfare?

title: The One Health Concept
content:
One Health links human, animal, and environmental health.
Farm-level misuse contributes to human infections through food chains.
Video: One Health in action – protecting all species.
Quiz: Why is antibiotic use in animals a global concern?
sms_content: One Health links human, animal, and environmental health. Farm-level misuse contributes to human infections through food chains. Video: One Health in action – protecting all species. Quiz: Why is antibiotic use in animals a global concern?

title: When to Use Antimicrobials
content:
Only use antimicrobials under veterinary prescription.
Treat bacterial infections – not viral, fungal, or parasitic conditions.
Interactive decision tree: When is antibiotic use justified?
Quiz: Can antibiotics cure viral diseases?
sms_content: Only use antimicrobials under veterinary prescription. Treat bacterial infections – not viral, fungal, or parasitic conditions. Interactive decision tree: When is antibiotic use justified? Quiz: Can antibiotics cure viral diseases?

title: Alternatives to Antimicrobials
content:
Good hygiene, vaccination, nutrition, and welfare reduce disease need for drugs.
Media: Farmer testimony on vaccination vs. treatment.
Quiz: Name two ways to reduce dependence on antibiotics.
sms_content: Good hygiene, vaccination, nutrition, and welfare reduce disease need for drugs. Media: Farmer testimony on vaccination vs. treatment. Quiz: Name two ways to reduce dependence on antibiotics.

title: Correct Dosage and Administration
content:
Follow veterinary dosage and full treatment duration.
Avoid underdosing and overuse – both encourage resistance.
Interactive simulation: Correct dose calculator.
Quiz: What happens when treatment is stopped early?
sms_content: Follow veterinary dosage and full treatment duration. Avoid underdosing and overuse – both encourage resistance. Interactive simulation: Correct dose calculator. Quiz: What happens when treatment is stopped early?

title: Storage and Disposal of Antimicrobials
content:
Store in cool, dry places; never share between animals.
Dispose of expired drugs safely – not in water or soil.
Infographic: Safe drug storage practices.
Quiz: Why should you avoid using leftover antibiotics?
sms_content: Store in cool, dry places; never share between animals. Dispose of expired drugs safely – not in water or soil. Infographic: Safe drug storage practices. Quiz: Why should you avoid using leftover antibiotics?

title: Understanding Withdrawal Periods
content:
Withdrawal period = time between last drug dose and food consumption.
Prevents residues in milk, meat, and eggs.
Interactive activity: Identify withdrawal times for common drugs.
Quiz: Why is respecting withdrawal time essential?
sms_content: Withdrawal period = time between last drug dose and food consumption. Prevents residues in milk, meat, and eggs. Interactive activity: Identify withdrawal times for common drugs. Quiz: Why is respecting withdrawal time essential?

title: Recognizing Antimicrobial Resistance
content:
Signs: repeated infections, reduced drug effectiveness, slow healing.
Video: Case example from poultry farm.
Quiz: How can farmers detect resistance early?
sms_content: Signs: repeated infections, reduced drug effectiveness, slow healing. Video: Case example from poultry farm. Quiz: How can farmers detect resistance early?

title: Farmers’ and Veterinarians’ Roles
content:
Farmers must follow vet instructions and keep treatment records.
Vets must diagnose, prescribe, and monitor treatment outcomes.
Media: Farm–vet collaboration success story.
Quiz: What are two farmer responsibilities in antimicrobial use?
💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning)
Each SMS lesson is under 480 characters and sent through text codes (e.g., WTS AM1–AM10). These concise lessons reach farmers without smartphones.
🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes
Languages: English, Luganda, Runyankole, Kiswahili
• Focus: Smallholder farmers, extension workers, and vet trainees
• Dissemination: App lessons, radio talk shows, SMS codes, and community vet clinics
• Oversight: Bugema University ethical review and AMR experts
• Partnership: WTS‑LEARN, FAO, WOAH, and local veterinary networks.
sms_content: Farmers must follow vet instructions and keep treatment records. Vets must diagnose, prescribe, and monitor treatment outcomes. Media: Farm–vet collaboration success story. Quiz: What are two farmer responsibilities in antimicrobial use? 💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning) Each SMS lesson is under 480 characters and sent through text codes (e.g., WTS AM1–AM10). These concise lessons reach farmers without smartphones. 🌍 Localization & Impl

title: Introduction to Vaccination
content:
Definition: Vaccination is the process of giving an antigen to stimulate immunity against disease.
Purpose: Protect animals from infectious diseases and prevent transmission.
Benefits: Improves productivity, welfare, and herd immunity.
Media: Intro video explaining how vaccines work.
sms_content: Definition: Vaccination is the process of giving an antigen to stimulate immunity against disease. Purpose: Protect animals from infectious diseases and prevent transmission. Benefits: Improves productivity, welfare, and herd immunity. Media: Intro video explaining how vaccines work.

title: Principles of Immunity
content:
Active immunity develops after vaccination or natural infection.
Passive immunity is transferred via colostrum or maternal antibodies.
Interactive diagram: innate vs. adaptive immune response.
sms_content: Active immunity develops after vaccination or natural infection. Passive immunity is transferred via colostrum or maternal antibodies. Interactive diagram: innate vs. adaptive immune response.

title: Types of Vaccines
content:
Live attenuated vaccines: contain weakened pathogens.
Inactivated vaccines: killed organisms or fragments.
Toxoids: inactivated toxins for diseases like tetanus.
Media: infographic showing examples and uses.
sms_content: Live attenuated vaccines: contain weakened pathogens. Inactivated vaccines: killed organisms or fragments. Toxoids: inactivated toxins for diseases like tetanus. Media: infographic showing examples and uses.

title: Handling and Storage of Vaccines
content:
Vaccines are sensitive to heat, light, and freezing.
Maintain the cold chain (2–8°C) from factory to farm.
Do not expose vaccines to sunlight or contaminated water.
Interactive checklist: Do’s and Don’ts of vaccine storage.
sms_content: Vaccines are sensitive to heat, light, and freezing. Maintain the cold chain (2–8°C) from factory to farm. Do not expose vaccines to sunlight or contaminated water. Interactive checklist: Do’s and Don’ts of vaccine storage.

title: Vaccine Administration
content:
Routes: intramuscular, subcutaneous, oral, or intranasal.
Use clean, sterile syringes and correct dosage per animal.
Follow manufacturer and veterinary instructions.
Video demonstration: proper vaccination technique.
sms_content: Routes: intramuscular, subcutaneous, oral, or intranasal. Use clean, sterile syringes and correct dosage per animal. Follow manufacturer and veterinary instructions. Video demonstration: proper vaccination technique.

title: Vaccination Schedules
content:
Follow national or veterinary vaccination calendars.
Repeat boosters as recommended to maintain immunity.
Infographic: species-specific vaccination timeline.
sms_content: Follow national or veterinary vaccination calendars. Repeat boosters as recommended to maintain immunity. Infographic: species-specific vaccination timeline.

title: Common Vaccines and Target Diseases
content:
Examples: FMD, LSD, Rabies, Newcastle Disease, Anthrax, Brucellosis.
Use approved vaccines only; avoid expired ones.
Interactive quiz: match vaccine to disease.
sms_content: Examples: FMD, LSD, Rabies, Newcastle Disease, Anthrax, Brucellosis. Use approved vaccines only; avoid expired ones. Interactive quiz: match vaccine to disease.

title: Adverse Reactions and Management
content:
Possible reactions: swelling, mild fever, lethargy.
Serious reactions: anaphylaxis—requires immediate vet attention.
Activity: identify early signs of vaccine reaction.
sms_content: Possible reactions: swelling, mild fever, lethargy. Serious reactions: anaphylaxis—requires immediate vet attention. Activity: identify early signs of vaccine reaction.

title: Record Keeping and Monitoring
content:
Keep vaccination records (animal ID, vaccine, date, vet name).
Evaluate vaccine effectiveness through follow-up inspections.
Template: digital vaccination record form in app.
sms_content: Keep vaccination records (animal ID, vaccine, date, vet name). Evaluate vaccine effectiveness through follow-up inspections. Template: digital vaccination record form in app.

title: Community Awareness and Ethics
content:
Encourage participation in vaccination campaigns.
Promote accurate information—avoid vaccine myths.
Ensure animal welfare and humane handling during vaccination.
Video: success story from rural community outreach.
💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning)
These short SMS lessons summarize the key ideas from the Vaccination module for farmers without smartphones. Each message fits within 480 characters and can be accessed by texting the corresponding code (e.g., WTS VAC1–VAC10).
🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes
Languages: English, Luganda, Runyankole, Kiswahili
• Target Users: Farmers, para-vets, and students in East Africa
• Integration: Radio shows, SMS codes, community training & WTS‑LEARN app
• Oversight: Bugema University ensures ethical review & welfare alignment
• Duration: Each lesson 3–5 min (app) or 1–2 min (SMS)
sms_content: Encourage participation in vaccination campaigns. Promote accurate information—avoid vaccine myths. Ensure animal welfare and humane handling during vaccination. Video: success story from rural community outreach. 💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning) These short SMS lessons summarize the key ideas from the Vaccination module for farmers without smartphones. title: Introduction to Wound Management
content:
Definition of a wound: any compromise to tissue integrity.
Wound management = actions to assist healing and prevent infection.
Wounds affect animal welfare and can indicate poor management.
Media: animated video of common animal wounds and welfare impacts.
Quiz: Why does wound care matter for animal welfare?
sms_content: Definition of a wound: any compromise to tissue integrity. Wound management = actions to assist healing and prevent infection. Wounds affect animal welfare and can indicate poor management. Media: animated video of common animal wounds and welfare impacts. Quiz: Why does wound care matter for animal welfare?

title: Stages of Wound Healing
content:
Four stages: Haemostasis, Inflammation, Proliferation, Remodelling.
Healing can be delayed by poor nutrition, infection, or stress.
Interactive timeline animation for each stage.
Activity: Match each healing stage with its description.
sms_content: Four stages: Haemostasis, Inflammation, Proliferation, Remodelling. Healing can be delayed by poor nutrition, infection, or stress. Interactive timeline animation for each stage. Activity: Match each healing stage with its description.

title: Assessing the Patient and the Wound
content:
Assess animal’s overall health (ABC: Airways, Breathing, Circulation).
Identify wound cause, type, location, and infection signs.
Observation game: identify wound types from images.
Quiz: What are signs of an infected wound?
sms_content: Assess animal’s overall health (ABC: Airways, Breathing, Circulation). Identify wound cause, type, location, and infection signs. Observation game: identify wound types from images. Quiz: What are signs of an infected wound?

title: Wound Types and Classification
content:
Types: abrasion, incision, laceration, puncture, avulsion, burn, pressure wounds.
Activity: Tap each image to see the wound type and treatment tip.
Media: photo guide for wound types.
Quiz: Which wound needs open management?
sms_content: Types: abrasion, incision, laceration, puncture, avulsion, burn, pressure wounds. Activity: Tap each image to see the wound type and treatment tip. Media: photo guide for wound types. Quiz: Which wound needs open management?

title: Step-by-Step Wound Treatment
content:
1. Restraint and handling; 2. Lavage; 3. Debridement; 4. Closure; 5. Bandaging.
Demonstration video on lavage and bandaging.
Tip: Always use antiseptic, nontoxic lavage fluids like 0.05% chlorhexidine.
Quiz: Why avoid alcohol or hydrogen peroxide on open wounds?
sms_content: 1. Restraint and handling; 2. Lavage; 3. Debridement; 4. Closure; 5. Bandaging. Demonstration video on lavage and bandaging. Tip: Always use antiseptic, nontoxic lavage fluids like 0.05% chlorhexidine. Quiz: Why avoid alcohol or hydrogen peroxide on open wounds?

title: Bandaging and Dressing Techniques
content:
Bandage layers: inner (moist), middle (padding), outer (adhesive).
Common dressings: honey, sugar, silver for antimicrobial action.
Interactive: drag layers to build a bandage.
Quiz: What’s the purpose of the inner bandage layer?
sms_content: Bandage layers: inner (moist), middle (padding), outer (adhesive). Common dressings: honey, sugar, silver for antimicrobial action. Interactive: drag layers to build a bandage. Quiz: What’s the purpose of the inner bandage layer?

title: Infection Control and Pain Management
content:
Signs: redness, heat, swelling, pus, smell.
Antimicrobials only when necessary – One Health approach.
Pain relief: analgesics, rest, gentle handling, cold therapy.
Quiz: Name one non-drug pain relief method.
sms_content: Signs: redness, heat, swelling, pus, smell. Antimicrobials only when necessary – One Health approach. Pain relief: analgesics, rest, gentle handling, cold therapy. Quiz: Name one non-drug pain relief method.

title: Enemies of Wound Healing
content:
Physical: pressure, motion, dead space.
Endogenous: disease, stress, poor nutrition.
Exogenous: poor hygiene, isolation, bad human–animal interaction.
Reflection: How can welfare improvements aid healing?
sms_content: Physical: pressure, motion, dead space. Endogenous: disease, stress, poor nutrition. Exogenous: poor hygiene, isolation, bad human–animal interaction. Reflection: How can welfare improvements aid healing?

title: Common Myths & Role of Animal Health Professionals
content:
Myths: motor oil or kerosene heals wounds – FALSE.
Role: educate caretakers, ensure proper hygiene, follow-up healing.
Video: field vet correcting misconceptions.
Quiz: What’s wrong with tight bandaging?
sms_content: Myths: motor oil or kerosene heals wounds – FALSE. Role: educate caretakers, ensure proper hygiene, follow-up healing. Video: field vet correcting misconceptions. Quiz: What’s wrong with tight bandaging?

title: Collaboration and Follow-Up
content:
Good healing depends on cooperation between vet and caretaker.
Record progress, provide aftercare advice, promote welfare improvements.
Upload case report feature in app.
💬 SMS-Based Microlessons (Offline Learning)
Each SMS lesson fits within 3–4 messages (≤480 characters). Users can text lesson codes (e.g., WTS WND1–WND10) to access short learning tips.
🌍 Localization & Implementation Notes
Languages: English, Luganda, Runyankole, Kiswahili.
• Regional Fit: Reflects rural animal care realities in Uganda and East Africa.
• Integration: Vet extension programs, mobile app, SMS gateways, and radio.
• Offline Access: SMS keywords (e.g., 'WTS WND5') retrieve tips.
• Oversight: Bugema University ensures quality, ethics, and field relevance.
